methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14), and that's the rank. True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates.

rankings.blurb: New season high at 139. CLAUDE beat Spoons 9-5-2 in Week 10 with the best offensive line any team has put up this year: .309/.385/.559 with 18 HR, 34 R, 38 RBI, 10 SB across 256 AB. Plus 8 W, 78 K, 3.21 ERA, 1.14 WHIP, 6 QS, 5 SV+H over 81.1 IP. Now leads the league in K and AVG, and ranks top-3 in nine of fourteen cats. The only soft cells are SV+H (8) and QS (6). Both are minor; the rest of the team is so deep that the model treats them as cap on a top-tier ceiling rather than structural weakness.

Won big over UBC 10-4-2 with one of the best offensive weeks of Burns' season (50 R, .295 AVG, .389 OBP) and a strong pitching line (69.1 IP, 4 W, 64 K, 1.07 WHIP, 8 SV+H). The K/BB rank moved to 1st in the league. Cumulative profile still leads in W and ranks 2nd in WHIP, SV+H, and K/BB, with top-4 marks in QS, OBP, AVG. The structural offensive weaknesses (SB, RBI, HR) are still there but the floor is the highest in the league after CLAUDE.

Held at #3 despite a 4-10-2 loss to Burns. Offense was strong (44 R, 13 HR, 47 RBI, .271/.360/.458) but the new SP rotation laid an egg in its second full week: 61.2 IP, 3 W, 78 K, 5.40 ERA, 1.46 WHIP, only 1 QS. The Yamamoto/Valdez/Suarez/Eovaldi/Sheehan group still hasn't settled. Cumulative profile leads the league in R, HR, RBI, SV+H, and OBP — five #1 cats — but the pitching ERA dropped to 12th and W/K/QS are all bottom-three. The trade impact is real but uneven; W11 is the test of whether the new staff stabilizes.

Up one despite losing to CLAUDE 5-9-2. Pitching held its season-long form (78 IP, 6 W, 82 K, 3.00 ERA, 1.12 WHIP, 5 QS) and now leads the league in ERA, WHIP, AND QS — three #1 rate-cat finishes. The offense had its best week in over a month: 40 R, 9 HR, 33 RBI, .276/.344/.470, 7 SB. If the bats are this good even occasionally, the structural offensive weaknesses (still 11th in HR, 10th in RBI) become survivable.

Down one despite an absolute demolition of ShawSwank 13-1-2 — biggest single-week matchup win of any team this year. Week 10 line: 35 R, 11 HR, 36 RBI, 5 SB, 71.2 IP, 6 W, 74 K, 3.01 ERA, 1.27 WHIP, 5 QS, 6 SV+H. Best actual matchup record in the league at 8-1-1. The model's #5 ranking on power feels light given the W-L, but the underlying ranks (no top-3 cumulative) explain the cap. The W-L might be ahead of the underlying performance.

Up one on the back of the best pitching week of Y&Y's season: 69.2 IP, 6 W, 54 K, **1.94 ERA**, 1.06 WHIP, 3 QS, 2 SV+H. Beat Polar 8-5-3. The offense was solid (38 R, 14 HR, 38 RBI, .239 AVG). Now ranked 2nd in R and RBI, 3rd in AVG, 5th in OBP — the bats are real. Pitching ranks remain bottom-tier overall (11 QS, 9 K, 8 ERA, 7 WHIP) but trending the right way. Three 100-power teams are now in a dead heat at #4-6.

Tied Balls 7-7-2. The bats finally erupted (.297/.377/.562, 16 HR, 45 R, 43 RBI — best HR week of the season for any team) but the pitching imploded (61.2 IP, 2 W, 50 K, 5.98 ERA, 1.48 WHIP, 2.17 K/BB). Cumulative profile still leads in SLG and ranks 2nd in AVG, 3rd in HR, but dead-last in K/BB, 10th in W, 9th in WHIP and ERA. The team is one good pitching week away from being a real top-5 contender.

Held at #8 after a 7-7-2 tie with PO' BOYD. Bats showed up (40 R, 11 HR, 39 RBI, .281 AVG) and the pitching was decent (69.2 IP, 6 W, 63 K, 4.26 ERA, 1.21 WHIP, 6 QS). Cumulative ranks now 2nd in W and QS, 4th in ERA and WHIP. Still dead-last in SV+H (3 last week, 0 the week before). The matchup record (1-6-3) reflects the structural issue: great pitching rates can't carry the team when SV+H and SB are anchored at the bottom.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Up one on the back of an elite pitching week: 70.1 IP, 7 W, 70 K, 2.18 ERA, 1.17 WHIP, 6 QS, 7 SV+H. The Sánchez/Castillo/Gausman rotation is finally working. Beat Duran 7-6-3. Cumulative ranks now 4th in K and K/BB and 5th in W. The offense remains the problem — 11th in AVG and SLG, 9th in HR and OBP. The trajectory has been positive for three straight weeks.

Up one despite losing to Camp 6-7-3. The Week 10 line was solid (.273 AVG, 73 hits, 9 HR, 41 RBI, 6 W, 52 K, 3.59 ERA). Now leads the league in SB and ranks 4th in RBI cumulatively. The structural weaknesses haven't moved — 12th in OBP, 11th in WHIP and SLG, 11th in K/BB — but recent form has been respectable. Two narrow strengths (SB, SV+H) keep capping the ceiling around #10.

Drops two after the worst single-week performance any team has posted: .182 AVG, 6 HR, 27 RBI, 3 SB — bottom-three across all four hitting cats. Lost to Caminero 1-13-2, the largest matchup blowout of the season. Cumulative profile now ranks last in HR, RBI, SLG, AVG, WHIP — five 12th-place finishes. The pitching is still respectable (3rd in SV+H, 4th in W, 5th in K and QS) but the offensive collapse is now structural, not just a bad week.

Stays last. Lost to Y&Y 5-8-3 with a thin line (29 R, 56 hits in 207 AB, 2 W, 4.18 ERA). At 1-9 the matchup record is now mathematically unrecoverable for a playoff push. The roster gutted by the May 20 trade (lost their entire SP rotation to UBC) still hasn't found stability. The bullpen is the one functional unit (top-4 in SV+H) but everything else is broken.
